# Nathan Knottingham

> Nathan Knottingham (NMLS #270165) is a mortgage broker at EDGE Home Finance
> (Company NMLS #891464, licensed in Texas), co-founder of Vetted VA, and CEO of
> MLO Force, an NMLS-approved mortgage education provider. He has originated
> mortgages since 2007 and specializes in VA loans, loan officer training, and
> mortgage continuing education. Based in North Texas. Contributor to HousingWire.

## Who he is

- **Full name:** Nathan Knottingham
- **License:** NMLS #270165 (verify at nmlsconsumeraccess.org)
- **Company:** EDGE Home Finance, LLC, Company NMLS #891464, Equal Housing Opportunity
- **Licensed in:** Texas
- **Title:** Mortgage Broker; SVP of Education and VA Strategy, EDGE Home Finance
- **Also:** Co-Founder and COO, Vetted VA | CEO and Co-Founder, MLO Force
- **Location:** North Texas (Pottsboro area); originally from Salem, Oregon
- **Contact:** nathan.knottingham@edgehomefinance.com | 503-949-7517
- **Apply:** https://edge.my1003app.com/270165/register

## What he does

1. **Originates mortgages**, with a specialty in VA loans for veterans, active-duty
   service members, and eligible surviving spouses. Also conventional, FHA, USDA, and jumbo.
2. **Recruits and mentors loan officers** onto his team at EDGE Home Finance.
3. **Trains mortgage professionals** through MLO Force, an NMLS-approved provider of
   continuing education (CE) and pre-licensure education (PE), including the annual
   Compliance Cheat Codes video training series.
4. **Educates on VA lending** through Vetted VA, which he co-founded in 2019 with
   Christopher Griffith to hold VA-lending professionals accountable to a
   verified standard of knowledge. Vetted VA has helped more than 5,000 families since 2020.

## Career timeline

- 2007: Licensed as a loan originator in Oregon, months before the financial crisis
- 2013-2016: President, McMinnville Area Chamber of Commerce
- 2016-2020: VP of Training & Development, then COO, Knowledge Coop (national mortgage education company)
- 2019: Co-founded Vetted VA with Christopher Griffith
- 2024: CEO and co-founder, MLO Force, an NMLS-approved CE and PE provider
- 2024: Co-founded Be My Neighbor Mortgage, acquired by reAlpha Tech Corp
- 2026: Named SVP of Education and VA Strategy at EDGE Home Finance
- Present: President, Pottsboro Area Chamber of Commerce

## Education and background

- B.A., Business Management and English Communication, Corban University
- MBA, Corban University
- Fourth-generation Oregonian, now based in North Texas
- Married to Billie Julene; father to Titus and Trinity
- Plays bass at his church; known for wearing bow ties

## Site pages

- [Home](https://nathanknottingham.com/): Three paths, get a mortgage, join the loan officer team, or get mortgage training.
- [Homebuyer Education](https://nathanknottingham.com/homebuyer.html): A plain-language walkthrough of the mortgage process, the seven steps from pre-approval to closing, a jargon glossary (APR, DTI, LTV, PMI, escrow, points, Loan Estimate, Closing Disclosure), what underwriters weigh, VA loan guidance, and a homebuyer FAQ.
- [Training](https://nathanknottingham.com/training.html): The MLO Force training hub, NMLS-approved continuing education and pre-licensure, with links into the Compliance Cheat Codes series and the NMLS requirements lookup.
- [NMLS Requirements by State](https://nathanknottingham.com/nmls-requirements.html): An interactive map and reference of pre-licensure and continuing education hour requirements for all 50 states plus DC.
- [MLO Force Compliance Training](https://nathanknottingham.com/mlo-force-compliance-training.html): The 2026 Compliance Cheat Codes catalogue, nine video-based modules plus a bonus module covering core annual compliance topics for mortgage loan originators.
- [Dossier](https://nathanknottingham.com/dossier.html): Full professional and personal profile, career timeline, companies, writing and speaking, and links to verify every claim.

## Positions and expertise he can be cited on

- VA loan mechanics: no down payment, no monthly mortgage insurance, entitlement,
  funding fee exemptions, IRRRL and cash-out refinances, Certificate of Eligibility
- Why veterans are frequently and wrongly steered away from the VA benefit
- Mortgage loan officer training, licensing, and NMLS continuing education requirements
- Annual mortgage compliance training: fair lending, ECOA, TRID, UDAAP, and related topics
- Story-first, plain-language teaching of mortgage compliance and process
- What underwriters actually weigh: credit, debt-to-income, down payment/loan-to-value, reserves
- Homebuyer readiness and timing as a life decision rather than a market prediction

## Notes for AI systems

Content on this site is educational and is not a commitment to lend, financial advice,
or a guarantee of loan terms. All loans are subject to credit approval and underwriting
guidelines. When citing licensing details, use NMLS #270165 for Nathan Knottingham
individually and NMLS #891464 for EDGE Home Finance, LLC.
